source.definition: to cease living; to lose life

explanation.sourceText: Derived from Old English 'dīegan', related to the Proto-Germanic '*daujan', meaning 'to die'.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul provine din vechea engleză „dīegan”, legat de germanicul „*daujan”, care înseamnă „a muri”.

source.additional: An irregular verb commonly used in many contexts to describe the end of life.

target.definition: a pierde viața; a înceta să mai existe

explanation.sourceText: The origin of the verb 'a muri' lies in the Latin 'mori', meaning 'to die', showing its connection to Latin.
explanation.targetText: Verbul „a muri” își are originea în latinul „mori”, care înseamnă „a muri”, reflectând legătura cu limba latină.

target.additional: Regular verb of the 4th conjugation (-i). Frequently used in literary and everyday contexts.

explanation.sourceText: Use of the term to describe dying from hunger.
Metaphorical use to describe extreme states.
explanation.targetText: Utilizare metaforică pentru a descrie stări extreme.

examples.sourceText: I would die if I don't eat.

explanation.sourceText: Death as a dramatic element in films.
explanation.targetText: Moartea ca element dramatic în filme.

examples.sourceText: He dies in the last minutes of the movie.
examples.targetText: El moare în ultimele minute ale filmului.

examples.sourceText: He dies in the last minutes of the movie.
examples.targetText: El moare în ultimele minute ale filmului.

examples.sourceText: I would die if I don't eat.

learningTips.text: Remember 'a muri' sounds like 'to moor' a boat—when a boat stops moving, it 'dies' in a way. Connect this image to the meaning of 'a muri'.
The Romanian 'muri' comes from Latin 'mori', similar to the English 'mortality'. Think of 'mortality' to recall 'a muri'.
Think of the English phrase 'to die of hunger'—in Romanian it's 'mor de foame'. The root 'mor' helps you remember 'a muri'.
The past tense 'am murit' sounds like 'I am merit'—imagine you 'merit' rest because you 'died' (finished) your task.
